Even after he rectifies the concealment of the name *Elokim*, through which the revelation of *Havayah* is drawn down, it is the revelation of *Havayah* as it is through the name *Elokim*. Yisrael, who is named for "for you have struggled with *Elokim*," for whom the concealment of the name *Elokim* does not exist from the outset, the revelation of *Havayah* that is drawn down through Yisrael is the revelation of *Havayah* itself. It is explained in *Likutei Torah* that the *avodah* during the six days of the week is (generally) the *avodah* of Yaakov—the *avodah* of clarifications, to rectify the concealment of the name *Elokim*. The *avodah* of Shabbos is in the aspect of Yisrael, for on Shabbos it is written, "And *Elokim* finished," meaning the attribute of *tzimtzum* and the concealment of the name *Elokim* ceased, and the revelation of *Havayah* was drawn down. Therefore, there is no need then for the *avodah* of clarifications, and the *avodah* of Shabbos is to delight in *Havayah*, which is above the *avodah* of clarifications.

The aspect of Yeshurun is even higher than the aspect of Yisrael. Yeshurun is from the word *shir* (song), something circular like a ring that has no beginning and no end. It is explained in *Likutei Torah* that the reason Yisrael are called by the name "Yeshurun" is due to the root of the souls as they arose in thought. It can be said that the difference between Yeshurun and Yisrael—even though the matter of Yisrael, being named for "for you have struggled with *Elokim*," is also due to the revelation of the root of the soul that is above being clothed in a body [as explained in many places that Yaakov is the soul clothed in a body, and Yisrael is the root of the soul that is above being clothed in a body]—is that Yisrael is the aspect of a son (as mentioned above). The term and matter of "son" applies specifically after the son is *drawn* from the father's mind. Yeshurun is the essence of the soul as it is rooted in His *Atzmus* (Essence), similar to the son as he is in the father's mind itself (before he is drawn). This is why Yeshurun is from the word *shir*, which has no beginning and no end, for in the level of the souls as they have already been drawn from the father's mind (even as they are above), there are differences in levels, and the matter of a song that has no beginning and no end is in the essence of the souls as they are rooted in *Ohr Ein Sof*. This is what is meant by "And there was a king in Yeshurun," and it is stated in *Likutei Torah* that this refers to Rosh Hashanah, "Make Me King over you," for the matter of "Make Me King over you" is through the revelation of the essence of the soul as it is rooted in the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof*.

This is the connection of these three matters in Yisrael (Yaakov, Yisrael, and Yeshurun) to the three matters in the Torah: the matter of the "inheritance" in the Torah, which is due to the clarifications [for through the clarifications of the Torah, one inherits the *oros* of *Tohu*, as mentioned in section 2], is due to the aspect of Yaakov, for the *avodah* of clarifications is in the aspect of Yaakov. The matter of "Prepare yourself to study Torah," through which Torah study *lishmah* draws down the revelation of the Torah as it is above (the *pnimiyus* of the Torah), is due to the aspect of Yisrael, for the Torah study of the aspect of Yisrael is (not to clarify clarifications, but) to draw down the revelation of *Elokus*. The matter of the "gift" of Torah, which is the drawing down of the *taanug* of Torah (which is above comprehension) that is above division, is due to the aspect of Yeshurun.

6) One must understand that according to the above explanation, the reason the Torah is called an "inheritance" is because the Torah *brings* one to an inheritance (for through the clarifications of the Torah, one inherits the *oros* of *Tohu*). The interpretation of "Torah... morashah" (in the simple sense, for "a verse does not depart from its simple meaning") is that the Torah itself is an inheritance.

It can be said that the matter of the "inheritance" of the Torah (according to the interpretation that the Torah itself is an inheritance) is even higher than the matter of the "gift" of the Torah. The fact that it is brought above from the *drushim* that an inheritance is lower than a gift is because an inheritance is due to the existence of the *heir* (who is a relative of the bequeather) and does not depend on the will of the bequeather, while a gift is due to the will of the *giver*. This matter applies to an inheritance that is (only) due to kinship. But in an inheritance whose matter is that the heir stands in the place of the bequeather (*and consequently* inherits his assets), it is not appropriate to say that it comes due to the existence of the heir, since the heir is not a separate existence from the bequeather but stands in the place of the bequeather. On the contrary, this inheritance is higher than a gift. A gift is due to the *will* of the giver, and in an inheritance (in this manner), the heir stands in the place of the bequeather *himself* (which is above his will). Based on this, one must say that the reason it is said regarding the inheritance of the Torah (which is even higher than the gift of the Torah) that it is "morashah kehilas *Yaakov*" is because Yisrael and Yeshurun are names of elevation, and therefore they relate to the matter of revelations, and the matter of an inheritance where the heir stands in the place of the bequeather himself is revealed in the name "Yaakov," as it is the name of the essence, in which all of Yisrael are equal.

One must understand that the matter of the essence of the soul, in which all of Yisrael are equal, is emphasized more (seemingly) in the name "Yeshurun," for Yeshurun is from the word *shir*, which has no beginning and no end. Yet, the name "Yeshurun" relates to the matter of the "gift" (of Torah), which relates to the *will* of the giver, and regarding the "inheritance," which relates to the bequeather himself, it is said "morashah kehilas Yaakov." This will be understood by prefacing what is written: "He chose for us our inheritance, the pride of Yaakov..." which states two matters here: "He chose for us"—the matter of choice; and "our inheritance"—the matter of inheritance. It can be said that the matter of the choice, that Hashem chose Yisrael, is that He chose *to desire* them. The matter of the inheritance, that the heir stands in the place of the bequeather, is that Yisrael are rooted in the *Atzmus* itself. Even though the matter of choice is due to the *Atzmus*, which is not compelled by anything and can desire or not desire, nevertheless, the matter of choice is (not regarding the *Atzmus* itself, but) regarding His will, for the fact that He chose Yisrael is that He chose *to desire* them [and another matter here is that the choice itself (which is the cause of the will) is the wonder of the *Atzmus* (which is not compelled by anything and can desire or not desire), and not the *Atzmus* itself].

Based on this, it can be explained that regarding the matter of a "gift," the name "Yeshurun" is used, and regarding the matter of an "inheritance," the name "Yaakov" is used. For a gift, which is due to the will of the giver (as mentioned above), comes due to the choice (that He chose to desire). As it is written in the blessing of the Torah, "Who chose us from all the nations (and therefore) gave us His Torah." And since the matter of choice is the wonder of the *Atzmus* that is not compelled by anything, therefore, the vessel for this is the matter of "Yeshurun," from the word *shir*, a circle that has no beginning and no end, the elevation and wonder of the essence of the soul, which is above the division of beginning and end. The matter of inheritance, that Yisrael are rooted in the *Atzmus* itself, is revealed in the name "Yaakov," the name of the essence of Yisrael themselves.

7) It can be said that the fact that in the verse "He chose for us our inheritance," it says "the pride of *Yaakov*," is due to the matter of "our inheritance," for the matter of inheritance relates to Yaakov. The request, "He chose for us our inheritance," is that the matter of "our inheritance"—the essence of the soul that is rooted in the *Atzmus* itself—should be drawn down in revelation. Just as on Rosh Hashanah the main emphasis is "our inheritance, the pride of Yaakov," but we ask that this matter be drawn down in revelation, so too it is on Simchas Torah, where the main emphasis is "*morashah* kehilas *Yaakov*." And it says "*kehilas* (congregation of) Yaakov," that due to the matter of Yaakov, all of Yisrael are one existence (*kahal*), similar to how on Rosh Hashanah "You are standing... all of you, as one." This is why the *mitzvah* of the day (of Simchas Torah) is in dancing, for dancing is with the feet, and in the feet themselves—in the heel (*akev*) within them (Yaakov is from the word *akev*), in which all of Yisrael are equal.

To add: the fact that all of Yisrael unite to be one existence, "kehilas Yaakov," is not because an elevation that is above division is felt in them [similar to the unity of "Yeshurun," from the word *shir*, which is through the revelation of the *makif* (encompassing light) that is above division], for Yaakov is from the word *akev* (heel), and in the heel, no elevation is felt, but rather an essential unity. It is the same regarding the unity of Yisrael in the matter of dancing on the heels. And through the matter of "kehilas Yaakov," the matter of *morashah*, the inheritance of the Torah, is revealed, that "Yisrael, the Torah, and Hashem are all one." For in the "gift" of the Torah, there are three matters: the giver, the receiver, and the gift (Torah), whereas in the matter of the "inheritance" of the Torah, Yisrael, the Torah, and Hashem are all one.

The drawing down of this matter (*morashah*, the inheritance of the Torah) on Simchas Torah is with great joy. And from Simchas Torah, this is drawn down for the entire year, that it should be a year of joy in Torah study without any obstacles or delays, for it is because of this that all of Yisrael yearned, etc., for the days of Moshiach, for then there will be no obstacle or delay to engaging in Torah and *mitzvos* as explained, until "the earth will be full of the knowledge of Hashem as the waters cover the sea," with the coming of our righteous Moshiach, truly soon.

## Simchas Torah, 5737

**"You have been shown to know that Hashem is the Elokim; there is none else besides Him."** This verse is the first of the seventeen verses recited before the Hakafos. It can be said that this verse is also the "head" of all seventeen verses, encompassing them just as the head encompasses all the limbs of the body. We must understand the connection between "You have been shown..." and the Hakafos. This requires further explanation in light of what the Rebbe Rayatz stated: that the purpose of reciting these verses before the Hakafos is to provide "reasons" for the joy of Shemini Atzeres and Simchas Torah [similar to the verses of Malchios, Zichronos, and Shofros]. Seemingly, what is the connection between "You have been shown to know..." and the joy of Shemini Atzeres and Simchas Torah? Especially considering that "You have been shown to know" took place at the time of the Giving of the Torah, which is on the festival of Shavuos.

**2.** This can be understood by prefacing what is explained elsewhere regarding the recitation of the verses of Malchios, Zichronos, and Shofros: the reason we must bring proof from the Torah for these matters is that they are extremely lofty concepts. From this, we can also understand regarding the recitation of the verses before the Hakafos: the fact that we must bring proof from the Torah for the matter of Hakafos is because it is an extremely lofty matter. As explained in *Likutei Torah*, the reason the Torah only explicitly mentions the wine libation, while the water libation is only a *Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai*, is that the water libation is an extremely lofty matter that cannot be clothed in the letters of the Written Torah. The fact that the matter of Hakafos is not even explicitly stated in the Oral Torah and is merely a custom is because the matter of Hakafos is even loftier than the water libation. This is also why the joy of *Simchas Beis HaShoeivah*, despite being a very great joy—to the point that "whoever has not seen the joy of *Simchas Beis HaShoeivah* has never seen joy in his life" —is nevertheless within the realm of measurement and limitation. This is also understood from the fact that at the joy of *Simchas Beis HaShoeivah*, only *Chassidim* and men of action would dance, while all the people would come to see and hear. The fact that there is a limitation regarding those who participate in this joy is because the entirety of this joy (even among the *Chassidim* and men of action) is within measurement and limitation. This is not the case on Simchas Torah, and especially during the Hakafos; the custom of Israel (which is Torah) is that all of Israel (men, women, and children) rejoice in it, because this joy is above measurement and limitation. [To add: the custom is for the second Hakafos (on the night of Simchas Torah) to be performed with even greater joy than the first (on the night of Shemini Atzeres), because when the joy is at the same level as the previous Hakafos, without addition or increase, it is not complete joy, as it has become "stale." The Alter Rebbe writes in his *Siddur* that even when he had already conducted the Hakafos with joy in his own *beis medrash*, if he came to another *beis medrash* where they had not yet finished the Hakafos, he would sing and rejoice with them as well.] It can be said that this is analogous to the *avodah* of "make Me King over you" on Rosh Hashanah, in which all of Israel are equal, as it is written, "You are standing today, all of you..." —on the day of Rosh Hashanah, all ten categories of Israel (from your heads, your tribes... to your woodchopper and your water-drawer) are united as one.

**3.** Now, in the unity of all the categories of Israel on Rosh Hashanah, there are two aspects. Each has its own unique quality, and they complete one another. The fact that there is a quality in the lower levels relative to the "heads" can be connected to the concept that "their end is embedded in their beginning" : specifically the lower levels (the end) are embedded in the beginning (meaning their root is in the beginning that precedes the *Seder Hishtalshelus*; the quality of the "heads" is that they are the beginning and the head of the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, while the root of the lower levels (the end) is in the beginning that precedes the *Seder Hishtalshelus*). There is another aspect to the unity of Israel on Rosh Hashanah: all of Israel are one *komah*, one entity that is above division, which has no head or end. As it is written, "And He was King in Yeshurun"—the fact that Israel is called by the name *Yeshurun*, from the word *shir* (song), is because they are like a circle (*shir* is a circular thing) which has no head or end. It is similar regarding the joy and dancing of the Hakafos of Shemini Atzeres and Simchas Torah, which contain an analogy to these two aforementioned aspects. The fact that the dance is with the feet, and the feet lift and elevate the head, is because "their end is embedded in their beginning"—meaning there is a division of head and foot, yet in the foot there is a matter that is higher than the head. And the fact that the manner of joy on Simchas Torah is (not through study and contemplation, but) by taking a *Sefer Torah* as it is, wrapped and covered in a mantle, and dancing with it, is because the joy of Simchas Torah is connected with the Torah as it is above revelation—the *makif* of Torah (which cannot be clothed in *Chochma*), the *Keter* of Torah, for a *makif* has no head or end. Based on this, we can explain the connection of the verse "You have been shown..." to the joy of the Hakafos, for the verse "You have been shown..." contains an analogy to these two aspects, as follows.

**4.** This can be understood by prefacing that in the distinction between *Atah* (You), which is second person (revelation), and *Hu* (He), which is third person (concealment), there are two interpretations. *Atah* is lower than *Hu*. *Hu*, the third person, is the light that is above revelation [generally, this is the *Ohr HaSoviv*, whose influence in the worlds is not in revelation but in concealment], and *Atah*, the second person (revelation), is the *Ohr HaMemalei* which is in revelation within the worlds. Yet, *Atah* is also higher than *Hu*. As is known regarding the concept of *Atah* "is Hashem alone" —which refers to the light that is above revelation—and *Atah* is the *Atzmus*, which is everywhere in revelation, for the "Name of Heaven" is common in everyone's mouth. This is also true regarding "You have been shown to know," for there are two interpretations of this. In *Likutei Torah*, *D'H V'Yadata*, it is explained that what is written, "And you shall know today and take to your heart that Hashem is the Elokim..." even though it was already said, "You have been shown to know that Hashem is the Elokim..." is because "You have been shown to know" is the knowledge of the *Memalei Kol Almin*, while the command "And you shall know..." is that the knowledge should be of the *Soviv Kol Almin*. In other places, it is explained that "You have been shown to know" refers to the *Atzmus*. As the Alter Rebbe explains : *Atah*—the *Atzmus* of the *Ein Sof Baruch Hu*—*ha'reisa* (You have shown) *az men zol dich visen* (so that one should know *You*). The fact that the *Atzmus* is in revelation (which is why we address Him as *Atah*, second person) is that He is in revelation just as He is in His *Atzmus*, and the fact that everyone knows that there is an existing *Eloka* is not yet "knowledge and comprehension." The possibility of having knowledge and comprehension in *Elokus* is because the revelation of the *Atzmus* at the time of the Giving of the Torah ("You have been shown") was in a manner of "to know," so that there could be knowledge of *Elokus* in general, and furthermore, that through this, it would be possible to have knowledge of the *Atzmus*—*az men zol dich visen*. And the verse continues, "that Hashem is the Elokim, there is none else besides Him," meaning that the knowledge of Him, *Yisbarach* (through "You have been shown to know"), is in two aspects: that Hashem is the Elokim, and that there is none else besides Him. We must understand: Hashem and Elokim are names, and a name is only an illumination; what is the connection of the knowledge that "Hashem is the Elokim" to "You have been shown" *az men zol dich visen*?

**5.** This can be understood according to what is explained in the *drushim* on the verse "You have been shown" : the matter of "You have been shown to know" is that He, *Yisbarach*, emanated the concept of knowledge within His *Atzmus* so that through this, there could be knowledge of *Elokus* in the created beings. It is explained in many places that the reason for the emanation of the ten *Sefiros* of *Atzilus* is so that through them there would be a revelation of *Elokus* in the world. For the essence of the revelation of *Elokus* in the world (internally) is primarily in the intellect and emotions of the created beings—the fact that created beings can know and comprehend *Elokus* and be moved by it. This matter is drawn from the ten *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*. By the "image" of intellect and emotions being formed, so to speak, in *Elokus* (*Atzilus*), the knowledge and emotional response to *Elokus* is drawn into the created beings (whose root is from the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*). The emotional response to *Elokus* in created beings is drawn from the emotions of *Atzilus*, and the knowledge and comprehension of *Elokus* in created beings is drawn from the intellect of *Atzilus*. The fact that it is explained in the *drushim* on "You have been shown to know" that in order for there to be knowledge of *Elokus* in created beings, He emanated the concept of knowledge in His *Atzmus* (even though, seemingly, the knowledge in the *Chabad* of *Atzilus* would have sufficed for created beings to have knowledge of *Elokus*), is because this is so that there could be knowledge, so to speak, even of the *Atzmus*—*az men zol dich visen*. Because the knowledge of the *Chabad* of *Atzilus* was drawn from the concept of knowledge in His *Atzmus*, therefore, the knowledge of *Elokus* of the created beings, which is drawn from the *Chabad* of *Atzilus*, is also connected to the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is above *Atzilus*, and even to the knowledge of the *Atzmus*.

"This is 'You have been shown to know that Hashem is the Elokim,'" for in the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus* (even in the *Chabad* of *Atzilus*) there are lights and vessels. The root of the lights is from *Hashem*, and the root of the vessels is from *Elokim*. Through the knowledge that "Hashem is the Elokim"—that *Hashem* and *Elokim* are one —one arrives at the knowledge that "there is none else besides Him." For the two names, *Hashem* and *Elokim*, as they are in themselves, are two different things, and furthermore, they are two opposite things: *Hashem* is revelation, and *Elokim* is *tzimtzum* (contraction) and concealment. The fact that *Hashem* and *Elokim* are one is because both are names of the *Hakadosh Baruch Hu* who is above both of them ; rather, it arose in His will, *Yisbarach*, that His revelation should be in the two lines of *Bli Gvul* and *Gvul* (revelation and *tzimtzum*). Therefore, even after they are drawn into revelation in the form of names, where they are two opposite things, they are integrated with one another, to the point that *Hashem* and *Elokim* are one.

**6.** The fact that the lights and vessels (*Hashem* and *Elokim*) are one in their root has several levels. Generally, this is the concept of the "hidden ten *Sefiros*." Even in the hidden *Sefiros* there are (analogous to) lights and vessels, and they are the root of the lights and vessels in the revealed *Sefiros*, except that in the hidden *Sefiros* they are one. Just as the division of the *Sefiros* (*Chochma*, *Binah*, etc.) exists in the revealed *Sefiros*, whereas in the hidden *Sefiros* they are all included as one in a state of intermingling and unity, so too is it regarding the division of lights and vessels; in the hidden *Sefiros*, they (the lights and vessels) are in unity. However, specifically, even in the hidden *Sefiros*, lights and vessels are two things. The fact that *Hashem* and *Elokim* are one is in the general light before the *tzimtzum*. It is known that in the light before the *tzimtzum* there are two general levels. The light in itself is *Ein Sof* [and the *Sefiros* that are from the light itself are *Sefiros Ein Ketz*], and it is the root of the *Ohr HaSoviv*. Because it arose in His will to emanate, etc., it also contains the root of the ten *Sefiros* (the hidden *Sefiros*), and it is the root of the *Ohr HaMemalei*. This is why His "Great Name" before the *tzimtzum* is analogous to *Hashem* with the vowels of *Elokim*: in itself it is *Ein Sof*—*Hashem*—yet it also contains the root of the light of limitation (*Memalei*), *Elokim*. Since the fact that it contains the root of the light of limitation is because it arose in *His will* (of the *Ohr Ein Sof Bli Gvul*) that there should be ten *Sefiros*—and the true nature of will is that even after He wills a certain thing, the essence of the thing He wills is (not the thing in itself, but) to complete *that which He wills* —therefore, the root of the light of limitation as it is before the *tzimtzum* is (not a thing in itself, but) one with (the will of) the *Ohr Ein Sof Bli Gvul*. This is also understood from what was brought above, that His Great Name before the *tzimtzum* is analogous to *Hashem* with the vowels of *Elokim*, meaning it is one name that includes the two aspects of *Hashem* and *Elokim*. However, more specifically, since even in the will there is a "taking of place" for the thing one wills (that specifically through this thing the will is completed), therefore, the fact that in the light before the *tzimtzum* there is a root for the *Ohr HaSoviv* (*Bli Gvul*, *Hashem*) and a root for the *Ohr HaMemalei* (*Gvul*, *Elokim*), they are like two things. The true meaning of *Hashem* and *Elokim* being one is in the aspect of "potential" (which is above the aspect of *Sefiros*, where even *Sefiros Ein Ketz* do not apply), for the fact that He has the potential to illuminate (the root of *Hashem*) and the potential not to illuminate (the root of *Elokim*) is truly one. This is similar to what is explained elsewhere, that in the light before the *tzimtzum*, even in the light that is before the arising of the will, revelation and concealment are two things. Therefore, the *avodah* as it is from the perspective of the light before the *tzimtzum* is to draw down an abundance of light (similar to how before the *tzimtzum* the power of revelation was stronger than the power of concealment). And in order for there to be *it'hafcha chashucha l'nehura* (the transformation of darkness into light), where the darkness itself is transformed into light, it is specifically from the aspect of "potential." Since the potential to illuminate (the root of light) and the potential not to illuminate (the root of darkness) are truly one, therefore, by reaching the aspect of potential, even the darkness is transformed into light.

"This is the connection of 'Hashem is the Elokim' with 'there is none else besides Him,'" for through the knowledge that "Hashem is the Elokim" one arrives at the knowledge that "there is none else besides Him" (as in section 5). From the fact that we see that *Hashem* and *Elokim* are truly united as one—that in the *avodah* of man, "And you shall love Hashem your Elokim with all your heart" means with both your inclinations, that even the *Yetzer Hara* (whose root is from the concealment of *Elokim*) loves *Hashem* (like the *Yetzer Hatov* whose root is from the revelation of *Hashem*)—from this we know that "there is none else besides Him," for the root of both aspects (of the revelation of *Hashem* and the concealment of *Elokim*) is that He has the potential to illuminate and the potential not to illuminate. The matter of "potential" is not a thing at all other than the *Atzmus*, and it is only that the *Atzmus* is "capable"—there is none else besides Him. This is also what is said at the beginning of the verse, "You have been shown to know," *du host zich bavizen az men zol dich visen*, because since the matter of "potential" is that the *Atzmus* is capable, the knowledge of the matter of "potential" is the knowledge of the *Atzmus*.

**7.** Based on this, we can also explain the connection of the two interpretations (in section 4 above) of "You have been shown to know": that "You have been shown to know" is the knowledge of the *Memalei Kol Almin*, and that "You have been shown to know" is the knowledge of the *Atzmus* (*az men zol dich visen*). For the knowledge and contemplation of the light that is clothed in the worlds (*Ohr HaMemalei*) is primarily so that even the intellect of the *nefesh habehamis* should know *Elokus*. The fact that even the intellect of the *nefesh habehamis* can understand *Elokus* (and in a way that through this it comes to *bitul ha-yesh*, the opposite of its nature) is through the revelation of the potential of the *Atzmus*, through which even the darkness is transformed into light. This knowledge is through the power of the *Atzmus*, but the knowledge itself is only in the *Ohr HaMemalei*. After this knowledge, there must be the knowledge and contemplation of the *Ohr HaSoviv* [which is the command "And you shall know," as in section 4], and that the *Ohr HaMemalei* that is clothed in the worlds is only an illumination that is incomparable to the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is above the worlds (*Ohr HaSoviv*). Then, specifically, it is appropriate to contemplate and know the superiority of the knowledge of the *Ohr HaMemalei*—which is also understood in the intellect of the *nefesh habehamis*—that this knowledge is from the potential of the *Atzmus*, as mentioned above.

**8.** Based on this, we can also explain what was brought above (section 5) from the *drushim*: that the reason He, *Yisbarach*, emanated the concept of knowledge in His *Atzmus* is so that through this, there could be knowledge of *Elokus* in created beings. From the fact that the intention in the emanation of knowledge in His *Atzmus* is so that created beings would know *Elokus*, it is understood that there is a superiority in this knowledge (that created beings know *Elokus*) relative to the knowledge that is in His *Atzmus*. This is similar to what is explained in *Tanya* that the purpose of the *Hishtalshelus* of the worlds is not for the sake of the higher worlds, since for them it is a descent from the light of His countenance, *Yisbarach*. The purpose of the higher worlds is that through them this lower world was created, through which the intention of "a dwelling place in the lower realms" is completed—a dwelling place for His *Atzmus*. Thus, the matter of the higher worlds, through which this lower world is created, is loftier than the higher worlds themselves. The higher worlds themselves are in a state of descent, and the fact that through them the lower world is created is their ascent. It is similar regarding the emanation of knowledge in His *Atzmus*: the knowledge is like a descent from the light of His countenance [for although the emanation of this knowledge is in His *Atzmus*, nevertheless, relative to the *Atzmus* as He is in Himself (before He emanated the knowledge), the knowledge is a descent]. The purpose of the emanation of knowledge is that through it there should be knowledge of *Elokus* in created beings, even in the intellect of the *nefesh habehamis*, through which the potential of the *Atzmus* is revealed. Through the drawing down from the knowledge of Himself to the knowledge of *Elokus* in created beings, an ascent is created, so to speak, in the knowledge of Himself.

**9.** It can be said that the ascent created in the knowledge of Himself through the knowledge of *Elokus* in created beings is analogous to the influence from the foot to the head, because the root of the foot is higher than the head—"their end is embedded in their beginning". This matter still belongs to the *Seder Hishtalshelus*. There is another aspect to "You have been shown to know": that through the knowledge of *Elokus* of the created beings, the revelation of "there is none else besides Him"—the potential of the *Atzmus*—is drawn down (afterwards). This is why before the Hakafos we recite "You have been shown to know... there is none else besides Him," which is an explanation of the greatness of the loftiness of the joy of the Hakafos. The fact that the joy is in a manner where the feet lift and elevate the head refers to the head of the person and the "head" in every single level, up to the level of the knowledge of Himself. Furthermore, through the dancing of Simchas Torah, the aspect of the *makif* that is above the definitions of head and foot is drawn down, up to the revelation of "there is none else besides Him," the potential of the *Atzmus*.

The vessel for these two matters is the unity of Israel in two ways: that everyone receives from their fellow, and that all of Israel are one *komah*. It is similar in the personal *avodah* of each individual, which contains these two matters: the *avodah* of one's ten powers of the soul (which are analogous to the ten categories from "your heads, your tribes... to your woodchopper and your water-drawer") in a manner where all the powers receive from one another and complete one another, and the *avodah* that is from the *Yechidah* which is above division. This is also a close preparation for the true and complete Redemption through *Mashiach Tzidkeinu*, for the essence of *Mashiach* is *Yechidah*. This is why in every single Jew there is a spark of *Mashiach* —the personal *Yechidah* in every Jew is a spark from the general *Yechidah* of *Mashiach* [similar to Moshe (the first redeemer)—in every Jew there is the aspect of Moshe within him]—may he come and redeem us and lead us upright to our land, in the true and complete Redemption, very soon indeed.

## Motzoei Simchas Torah – In the Rebbe’s Room – 5738

To understand the concept of Simchas Torah, we must note the well-known point regarding the manner of this joy: we take a Sefer Torah, wrapped and covered in its mantle, and dance with it. Although it is written in the Torah, "For it is your wisdom and your understanding," nevertheless, this specific joy is not achieved through study and intellectual analysis, but specifically through dancing. This is why the joy of Simchas Torah is accessible to all Jews, even the simplest among them who are not involved in intellectual comprehension [and furthermore, during the joy and dancing of the Hakafos, even those who do not show a connection or unity with the matters of Torah during the rest of the year are drawn in, as we see tangibly]. This concept—that the joy of Simchas Torah belongs to every single Jew—is also expressed by the fact that we dance with the Sefer Torah, the Written Torah, for even one who does not know what he is saying recites the blessing over the Torah when he is called to the Written Torah. Since this blessing is recited by the *command* of the Torah of *Truth*, it is clear that this is the reality: even one who does not know what he is saying draws down the Ohr Ein Sof and even the Essence and Being (the Giver of the Torah) into the Torah he is studying. It is also well known that we must consider why Simchas Torah is celebrated on Shemini Atzeres (and in the Diaspora, on the second day of Shemini Atzeres), when, on the face of it, it should have been on Shavuos, the time of the giving of our Torah.

2) The explanation for this is brought based on the Midrash: "The Atzeres of the Festival (Sukkos) should have been fifty days away, like the Atzeres of Pesach (Shavuos). However, because they are transitioning from summer to winter, it is not a time when they can easily come and go. It is like a king who had many daughters; some were married nearby and some far away. One day, they all came to greet their father the king. The king said, 'Those who live nearby can come and go, but those who live far away cannot. Until they are all here, let us make one festival and rejoice together.' So too, regarding the Atzeres of Pesach, as they are transitioning from winter to summer, the Holy One, Blessed be He, said, 'It is a time to come and go.' But regarding the Atzeres of the Festival, as they are transitioning from summer to winter, the Holy One, Blessed be He, said, 'It is not a time to come and go; until they are all here, let us make one festival and rejoice.'" This is why we celebrate Simchas Torah on Shemini Atzeres: the "one day" on which all the daughters [the daughters of Zion and Jerusalem, meaning the souls of Israel] come to greet their father the King refers to Yom Kippur. Following this, the statement "let us make one festival and rejoice" refers to Simchas Torah (which is set on Shemini Atzeres), which is a "*one* festival" analogous to the "one day" of Yom Kippur.

3) This can be understood based on what was explained previously (in the *maamar* "B'Sukkos Teishvu") that all the festivals of the month of Tishrei are in the category of "one" (*achas*). Regarding Rosh Hashanah, our Sages say that the mitzvah of the day is the Shofar. This means that what distinguishes the day of Rosh Hashanah from other festivals is one single mitzvah: the mitzvah of Shofar. Regarding this mitzvah itself, the Rambam writes that it is a divine decree beyond human reason. Even though the mitzvah of Shofar also hints at the most sublime matters of *teshuvah* (as the Rambam continues, "there is a hint in the matter..."), its essence is a divine decree beyond reason. The same applies to Yom Kippur [the conclusion of the ten days between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur], which is the category of "one" for all three realms: *olam* (world), *shanah* (year), and *nefesh* (soul). The special *avodah* of Yom Kippur takes place in the Holy of Holies, the "one" of *olam* (place); on the day of Yom Kippur, which is called "one in the year," the "one" of *shanah* (time); and it is performed by the *Kohen Gadol* [who acts as the emissary for all of Israel], of whom it is written "And Aharon was set apart to sanctify him as Holy of Holies," the "one" of *nefesh*. The same applies to Sukkos, where the mitzvah of dwelling in the Sukkah [which lasts for all seven days of Sukkos, from the very first moment] emphasizes the unity ("one") of all Israel, as our Sages say, "All of Israel are fit to sit in one Sukkah." Likewise, this is true for the mitzvah of taking the Four Species [whose purpose is to draw the *makif* (the "one Sukkah") into the *pnimiyus* (internalization)]. In each of these four species, the concept of unity is evident and emphasized, especially in the *esrog*, where the unity is emphasized even more [which is why in the order of the Four Species in the Torah (and the order in the Torah is itself Torah), the beginning is with the *esrog*, the "fruit of a beautiful tree"]. The *esrog* is called in the verse "fruit of a beautiful tree" (*pri etz hadar*) because it "dwells" (*dar*) on its tree from year to year; meaning, it endures the changes of the four climates throughout the year, to the point that (not only does it endure all the climates, but) it grows from them. The quality of unity in the *esrog* is also drawn into the other three species, which is why the "beauty" (*hadar*) of the other three species is learned from the *esrog*. Furthermore, while each of the four species indicates unity, the mitzvah of taking the Four Species is to unite all four, which represent the four types within the Jewish people, so that they may all become one single bond to do Your will with a whole heart.

4) Now, for the true (and lasting) unity of all four types of Jews to be possible, it must be through *teshuvah*. The essence of *teshuvah* is above division, which is why it happens "in one hour and in one moment." This is the difference between the first Tablets (the aspect of Torah by itself) and the second Tablets (the aspect of Torah through *teshuvah*): to receive the first Tablets, Moshe was on the mountain for forty days, in addition to the preparation of "count for yourselves seven weeks" that preceded it. Not so with the second Tablets; the forty days Moshe spent on the mountain were (primarily) to ensure that the Holy One, Blessed be He, would be reconciled with Israel with joy and a whole heart, and after that, the Tablets were given "in one hour." This matter [that *teshuvah* is "in one hour and in one moment"] emphasizes that *teshuvah* is above all measurement and limitation, because true *teshuvah* reaches the *pnimiyus* and essence of the soul, a level that is above division—the "one" (*achas*—"in one hour and in one moment") of the soul. This is why, through *teshuvah*, the unity of all types of Jews is achieved, "from your heads, your tribes... to your woodchopper and your water-drawer," in the manner of "You are standing today, all of you." "Standing" (*nitzavim*) is related to the word for a king (*natzav*), meaning that Jews become kings and masters over the entire reality of the world. This begins with them being written and sealed for a good, sweet year, in a goodness that is visible and revealed below ten *tefachim*, even in physical matters, because from the perspective of this level, there is no distinction between spirituality and physicality, or between above and below; this permeates the entire *Seder Hishtalshelus* and even that which is above the *Seder Hishtalshelus*.

This is the meaning of "One day they all came to greet their father the King... let us make one festival and rejoice." The "one day" (on which they all came to greet their father the King) is Yom Kippur, the "one" of the year (and also the "one" of the world and the soul). From this is drawn the subsequent "let us make one festival," which refers to Simchas Torah, which is also a "one day" (*chad yoma*), the category of "one."

5) The arousal of the "one" level in the soul, which is above division, must be drawn into the levels of the soul that are within the realm of division [analogous to how this arousal must be connected to physical action, such as the action of sitting in a Sukkah, taking the Four Species, or dancing with one's feet]. This is why, after it is said, "You are standing today, all of you," indicating that all of Israel are in the state of "all of you" due to the "one" level within them, it then lists all ten types in detail, "from your heads, your tribes... to your woodchopper and your water-drawer." This means that the "all of you" (the "one" level) of Israel must be evident in them even as they exist in the state of division. Just as it is in the soul, so it is in the year: the "one day" level of Yom Kippur and Simchas Torah leads and directs all the days of the year (starting from *Isru Chag*). In all the details of one's actions ("in all your ways"), it is evident that he has just come from the "one day" of Yom Kippur and Simchas Torah. This applies throughout the entire year; even though many days, weeks, and months have passed since Yom Kippur and Simchas Torah, the arousal of the "one day" does not weaken at all and remains in its full strength, because it comes from a level that is above division and change—the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed." By drawing the "one day" level, which is above division, into all the days of the year, which are in the realm of division and change, an elevation is achieved even in the "one day" level itself, similar to what was explained regarding the "fruit of a beautiful tree," which not only endures all the changes of the climates but grows from them.

6) May it be His will that all the above be realized in each and every one within the entirety of Israel, that it be a good and blessed year with goodness that is visible and revealed below ten *tefachim*, with children, life, and sustenance, and in abundance—both children, life, and sustenance in their literal sense, and children, life, and sustenance in their spiritual sense (from which the literal matters are derived). May it be that, out of joy and a good heart, immediately after Simchas Torah, the *avodah* of "And Yaakov went on his way" will take place, even regarding mundane matters [for the "Yaakov went on his way" after Yom Kippur refers to his "way" in the matter of *mitzvos*, as during the four days between Yom Kippur and Sukkos, all of Israel are occupied with *mitzvos*. Not so the "Yaakov went on his way" after Simchas Torah, which refers to his "way" even in mundane matters]. May every day—both weekdays, and certainly Shabbos and Yom Tov (including Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur)—be permeated in all its aspects, both general and specific, with the essential and internal point of the *pnimiyus* and essence of the soul, which is connected to the *pnimiyus* and essence of the Ein Sof, Blessed be He.

7) Since this comes from Simchas Torah, where joy breaks through all boundaries, above measurement and limitation, it breaks and nullifies all the boundaries and limitations of the inner *galus*, as well as the boundaries and limitations of *galus* in its literal sense. We shall merit that very soon our eyes will behold Your return to Zion in mercy, in the true and complete Redemption through Mashiach Tzidkeinu. He will come and lead us upright to our land, speedily in our days, truly; he will build the Beis Hamikdash in its place and gather the outcasts of Israel, etc. (to the point that) then "I will turn to the peoples a clear language... to serve Him with one shoulder," and the kingdom will be Hashem's.

## Shemini Atzeres, 5740

On the eighth day, it shall be an Atzeres for you. Our Rebbes, the Nesi’im, have noted the nuances of this verse. Shemini Atzeres is a festival in its own right regarding the laws of *Pazar Kashev*. If so, it should have said, "on the twenty-second day of the seventh month," just as it does for the other festivals where the days of the month are specified (e.g., "on the first day of the seventh month," "on the tenth day of the seventh month," and "on the fifteenth day of the seventh month"). Why does it say, "on the eighth day"?

Although Shemini Atzeres follows the seven days of Sukkos, and it is known that on Shemini Atzeres the matters of the seven days of Sukkos are "arrested" (gathered) and absorbed, the matters of Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur are also gathered and absorbed on Shemini Atzeres. Nevertheless, it is not called by a name that indicates its connection to (or that it follows) Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur. Why, then, is it called "Shemini" (Eighth)?

This is especially true according to the Baal Shem Tov’s teaching that the name given to a thing in the Holy Tongue indicates its essence, even pointing to its vitality and existence. From this, we understand that the primary essence of Shemini Atzeres (being called *Shemini* Atzeres) is that it follows the seven days of Sukkos.

We must also understand the phrase, "It shall be an Atzeres for you." The Midrash states, "This is what is written: 'They shall be for you alone, and no strangers with you.'" This involves two concepts. The concept of "for you alone" is "Yisrael and the King alone." Yet, the very fact that it must be excluded—"no strangers with you"—implies that there is room to think that Shemini Atzeres might also be for strangers, and therefore it must be excluded to say it is only "for you."

Furthermore, we must understand why Shemini Atzeres includes Simchas Torah, to the point that in Eretz Yisrael—"the land which Hashem your G-d always seeks, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year"—Shemini Atzeres and Simchas Torah are on the same day. Even in the Diaspora, where they are two days, Simchas Torah is on the second day of Shemini Atzeres. There is a well-known question regarding why Simchas Torah was established on Shemini Atzeres; seemingly, it should have been on Shavuos, the time of the giving of our Torah.

It is explained in many places that the reason we celebrate Simchas Torah on Shemini Atzeres is because of the *second* Luchos, which were given on Yom Kippur. But this itself—why the joy of Simchas Torah is specifically for the second Luchos and not the first—requires explanation. The primary revelation and perfection of the giving of the Torah occurred specifically with the first Luchos. This is true regarding Yisrael, who were then in a state of ultimate perfection—at the standing at Har Sinai, their filth ceased—and it is true regarding the world, which then experienced a literal revelation of Ohr Ein Sof below, as it is written, "And Hashem descended upon Har Sinai," which the Targum translates as "and He was revealed." This revelation affected the entire world, as our Sages say: "No bird chirped, no fowl flew, no ox lowed," and the voice of the Ten Commandments was heard from all four directions, from above and from below. The world (and all the *vav katzvos* within it) cried out, "I am Hashem your G-d," "You shall have no other gods." All of this was specifically with the first Luchos. Why, then, was Simchas Torah established on Shemini Atzeres specifically for the second Luchos?

The explanation given in many places—that we celebrate Simchas Torah on the second Luchos because it is written regarding them, "And He told you the hidden things of wisdom, for they are double to *tushiyah* (wisdom/counsel)"—requires clarification. Since the revelation of the giving of the Torah in Yisrael and the world occurred specifically with the first Luchos, as mentioned, the joy of Simchas Torah—which is expressed in songs and dances (the aspect of revelation)—should seemingly be on Shavuos, when the first Luchos were given in revelation and publicity.

This is especially true according to what is explained regarding the dances of Simchas Torah: that the essence of dancing is in the feet, and specifically in the heel, the lowest part of a person (though through this, the head is also lifted and dances). From this, it is understood that the joy of Simchas Torah is not primarily due to the Torah’s effect on Yisrael, who are the "head," but rather due to the Torah’s effect on the world, which is secondary to Yisrael—like the feet are secondary to the head—and within the world itself, in its lowest matters (the level of the heel). Based on this, it is even less understood why Simchas Torah was established specifically on the second Luchos, as the superiority of "double to *tushiyah*" in the second Luchos is within Yisrael.

It is also unclear why, according to this (that the joy of Simchas Torah is for the second Luchos given on Yom Kippur), they should not have established Simchas Torah the day after Yom Kippur. The reason they did not establish it on Yom Kippur itself could be because there is no joy without eating and drinking, but it should have been immediately after Yom Kippur. Perhaps we can say even more: we find that in the year of the dedication of the Beis Hamikdash, Yisrael did not observe Yom Kippur, and a Heavenly Voice went out and said to them, "You are all invited to the life of the World to Come," because the joy of the dedication of the Beis Hamikdash is higher than the affliction of Yom Kippur. Since the joy of Simchas Torah is an extremely great joy—greater even than the joy of Simchas Beis Hashoeivah, of which it is said, "He who has not seen the joy of Simchas Beis Hashoeivah has not seen joy in his life"—it would have been appropriate to establish Simchas Torah on Yom Kippur itself.

This can be understood by first introducing the concept of the first Luchos and the second Luchos in the *avodah* of man. The primary intent of the giving of the Torah is that there should be a receiving of the Torah, the *avodah* of man. The first Luchos represent the *avodah* of the *tzaddikim*, and the second Luchos represent the *avodah* of the *baalei teshuvah*. The superiority of the second Luchos over the first is because the *avodah* of *teshuvah* is higher than the *avodah* of the *tzaddikim*.

The concept is that the general difference between the first and second Luchos is that the giving of the first Luchos was from above—"And Hashem descended upon Har Sinai." Even though the revelation of the giving of the Torah affected the world and even the inanimate objects within it, to the point that the world became permeated by the revelation, this did not create a permanent refinement and elevation of the world; rather, the revelation from above merely shone into it. Likewise, regarding Yisrael, the fact that the revelation of the giving of the Torah affected the body (that their filth ceased) was due to the soul. At the time of the giving of the Torah, there was a revelation of the essence of the soul, the level of *yechidah* (which is not an existence unto itself, and its entire existence is that it is a vessel and receiver from the level of the *Yachid*). Since the *yechidah* is connected to the body, the revelation of the *yechidah* shone into the body, even into the level of the "heel" within it, similar to what is said regarding Avraham: "Because (*eikev*) Avraham listened to My voice."

The reason for this is that the revelation of the giving of the Torah was by way of *is’arusa d’leila* (arousal from above), not through *is’arusa d’letata* (arousal from below). Although there was the *avodah* of Sefiras HaOmer during the seven weeks between the Exodus and the giving of the Torah, it is understood that through this *avodah* (during the 49 and 50 days), they were not worthy of being a vessel for the revelation of the giving of the Torah. Rather, the revelation was by way of *is’arusa d’leila* from His own initiative—"I am the One who begins." The *avodah* required before the giving of the Torah was merely by way of preparation.

This is similar to what is explained in many places regarding the difference between an *is’arusa d’letata* that is commensurate with the *is’arusa d’leila*, and an *is’arusa d’leila* that comes as a gift, which no *is’arusa d’letata* can reach at all. Even though the *is’arusa d’leila* that comes as a gift also follows the preceding *is’arusa d’letata*, it is not that the *is’arusa d’letata* draws it down; rather, the drawing down of this *is’arusa d’leila* is from a level that no *is’arusa d’letata* can reach. The reason it follows the preceding *is’arusa d’letata* is because "the Holy One, blessed be He, only rests in a complete place."

It is the same regarding the *avodah* during the seven weeks before the giving of the Torah: the *avodah* was not a vessel for the revelation of the giving of the Torah (and it is not appropriate to say that the *avodah* drew down the revelation), but only by way of preparation, whereby through their *avodah* they became a "likeness" of a vessel (but not a literal vessel) for the revelation. This itself—that they were a likeness of a vessel—was due to their root and source, the Patriarchs and Matriarchs, "Avraham was one," which is why there is a view in the writings of the Arizal that the giving of the Torah began in the days of the Patriarchs. All of this was with the first Luchos.

Conversely, the giving of the second Luchos was through *is’arusa d’letata*. The *avodah* of *teshuvah* of Bnei Yisrael (regarding the sin of the Golden Calf) was an *is’arusa d’letata* that awakened and drew down the revelation of the second Luchos. This is why the giving of the second Luchos was in the month of Tishrei, because the matters of the month of Tishrei come through the *avodah* of Elul, which is a vessel and an *is’arusa d’letata* that awakens and draws down the matters of Tishrei. Therefore, through this, a refinement and elevation is created in the "below" itself (unlike the first Luchos). This is why the first and second Luchos in the *avodah* of man are the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* and the *avodah* of *teshuvah*: the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* (including the drawing down that comes through *avodah*) is in the category of "straight light," from above to below, whereas through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, a new thing is accomplished: the "below" itself is refined and elevated.

The concept is that the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* is when a person is in the state of "G-d made man straight," where every Jew, in whatever situation he desires, is by nature inclined to fulfill the will of his Creator (as in the well-known ruling) through Torah study, the performance of *mitzvos*, and the *avodah* of prayer. Therefore, through the *avodah* of *tzaddikim*, no true innovation is created, since the *avodah* is according to their natural disposition. Just as the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* is not a true innovation in man because the *avodah* is according to his natural disposition, so too regarding matters above: since the intent of creation (from the outset) is to do good to His creations, etc., through their *avodah* in Torah and *mitzvos*, this is due to the "nature" of the Good to do good.

It is the same even higher, in the level of the root of the souls as they are rooted in His Essence, may He be blessed, according to the teaching of the Maggid regarding "With whom did He consult? With the souls of the *tzaddikim*." It can be said that this matter is in His Essence, may He be blessed (above the level of the "nature" of the Good to do good), for since the pleasure He would take in the *avodah* of the *tzaddikim* already arose before Him, may He be blessed, from the beginning, then through the *avodah* of the *tzaddikim* (in practice), this is not a true innovation.

All this is regarding the *avodah* of *tzaddikim*, as per the precise language of our Sages, "With whom did He consult? With the souls of the *tzaddikim*." However, the true intent (which is even above the level of "With whom did He consult") is as it is written, "which G-d created to do"—to do, to rectify—that man, through his *avodah*, should create an innovation in creation, so that there will be (not only "These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created"—*toldos* written fully, the world was created in its fullness—but also) "These are the generations of Peretz"—*toldos* written fully—higher than "These are the generations of the heavens and the earth." Peretz is Mashiach, of whom it is said, "The breaker is gone up before them," at which time the world will be higher than it was at the beginning of creation when the world was created in its fullness (as in "new heavens and a new earth").

It is the same regarding the souls of Yisrael: the souls of the future will be higher even than Adam HaRishon as he was before the sin, as explained in *Likutei Torah* in its conclusion, regarding the "new souls" that will be in the future, which are higher than all the souls that were included in Adam HaRishon. This innovation is accomplished specifically through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, for this is why Mashiach (specifically) "comes to bring the *tzaddikim* back in *teshuvah*." Through this, the true intent is completed, which is above the matter of "With whom did He consult with the souls of the *tzaddikim*."

It is the same with *teshuvah* in its simple sense. When a person has sinned, blemished, and gone off the path (which is the opposite of man’s nature and the nature from above) and then rectifies this through *teshuvah*—especially when the *teshuvah* is in a manner where "willful sins become for him as merits"—a new thing is created that is above the nature of man and the nature from above (the nature of the Good to do good), and even above the root of the souls as they are in the level of "With whom did He consult."

The concept is that the *avodah* of *teshuvah* is by way of a "leap," where a person changes and transforms (not in order and sequence) and becomes a new existence. This is why *teshuvah* is above time; therefore, even as it is drawn into the level of time, it is "in one hour and in one moment," because the *avodah* of *teshuvah* is not in sequence. This is also why it is said regarding the *baal teshuvah*, "And it shall be that before they call, I will answer." This matter (answering before the call) is the opposite of nature and *hishtalshelus* (the chain of progression), because through the leap (of *teshuvah*) that is not in the order of the *hishtalshelus* within man, through this, it is also created above not in the order of *hishtalshelus*.

This is similar to what is written, "From the depths I have called You, Hashem." "From the depths" (plural) is from depth to depth; through the *avodah* of *teshuvah* from the deep inner point of the heart, through this he calls and draws from the depth and inwardness of the Ein Sof, blessed be He (to the point that the matter of *teshuvah* reaches His Essence, may He be blessed, as is known regarding the question asked of the Holy One, blessed be He, etc., and He said, "Let him do *teshuvah* and he will be atoned"). Through this drawing down, "willful sins become for him as merits." Just as through *teshuvah* a new thing is created in man, that he becomes a new existence, as mentioned, so too a new thing is created in the world, that willful sins are transformed into merits. These merits (that are transformed from willful sins), since they are created through the leap of *teshuvah*, are higher than the merits of *tzaddikim*.

This is why it is written regarding the second Luchos (which in the *avodah* of man is the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, as mentioned) "double to *tushiyah*." The intent of "double" is not merely double in number, but a much greater abundance, to the point of being incomparable (as is also understood from the fact that in the second Luchos, a multitude of matters were added: *halachos*, *midrash*, and *aggados*). For in addition to the fact that in the *baal teshuvah* there are two types of merits (double in number)—the merits of Torah and *mitzvos* (like in *tzaddikim*) and the merits created from the transformation of willful sins—the merits created from the transformation of willful sins are higher, to the point of being incomparable, to the merits of *tzaddikim*. Furthermore, even the Torah and *mitzvos* after the *teshuvah* are higher, with greater strength.

This is also one of the explanations for why the future redemption will be through the prior *avodah* of *teshuvah* specifically, for the attainment of Torah in the future will also be "double to *tushiyah*." And not just double in number—the attainment of everything they have learned now in the revealed Torah and the inner Torah (and his learning is in his hand) and the attainment in the Torah of Mashiach—but also to a much greater abundance, to the point of being incomparable. For at the coming of Mashiach, there will be "Let Him kiss me with the kisses of His mouth," where the secret of its reasons and the hidden treasures (the hidden things of wisdom) will be revealed. Furthermore, through this, the attainment of the revealed Torah will also be in a higher manner. It is the same through the *avodah* of Torah and *mitzvos* during the time of exile, for the general *avodah* during the time of exile (including the *avodah* of Torah and *mitzvos*) is the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, and through *teshuvah* comes "double to *tushiyah*."

This can be understood based on the well-known fact that the difference between the Babylonian Talmud and the Jerusalem Talmud is like the difference between the first Luchos and the second Luchos. The Jerusalem Talmud is by way of "straight light" (like the *avodah* of *tzaddikim*, the first Luchos), and the Babylonian Talmud is the *pilpul* with a multitude of questions and concealments—"He has made me dwell in darknesses" is the Babylonian Talmud. And yet, the *halachah* is specifically like the Babylonian Talmud, because through the toil in clarifying the questions, etc., one reaches the true intent, the level of the essential concealment that is in the Torah, the level of the "hidden things of wisdom" (like the superiority of the merits of the *baal teshuvah* that are created from willful sins over the merits of *tzaddikim*).

This is why it is written specifically regarding the second Luchos, "And He told you the hidden things of wisdom, for they are double to *tushiyah*." The "hidden things of wisdom" is (up to) the inwardness of the *Chochmah* of the *Ein Sof*, and at its root, it is the inwardness and essence of the *Ein Sof*, and this is revealed specifically through the *pilpul*, etc., of the Babylonian Talmud. This revelation will be primarily in the future, as it is found in the Zohar on the verse, "No eye has seen, etc., He will do for those who wait for Him." What is "for those who wait for Him"? Those who press for the word of wisdom and are precise about it and wait for it to know the clarification of the matter, etc. Through the multitude of study and toil in the multitude of questions, etc., there will be (in the future) the revelation of the level of Eden, of which it is said, "No eye has seen," the level of the essential concealment.

Through this, an elevation and perfection is created also in the Torah, that it becomes connected with the Essence (like David, who would connect the Torah from above with the Holy One, blessed be He). For the Torah in itself (as it was given at the giving of the Torah, in the first Luchos) is the level of *Chochmah*, for "the Torah emanates from *Chochmah*." And although at the giving of the Torah, the Torah that will be revealed in the future was also given (for the giving of the Torah was only once), at the time of the giving of the Torah, this was given in a state of concealment—"hidden things of wisdom"—and in order to draw this down into revelation (in the manner of Torah as "light"), this power was given specifically in the second Luchos.

This is why through the engagement in Torah specifically during the time of exile (Babylonian Talmud), there will be the revelation of the future, at which time the intent of a "dwelling place in the lower worlds" will be completed. For in a dwelling place in the lower worlds, there are two matters: a dwelling for His Essence, and a dwelling in the lower worlds, and these two matters are accomplished through the engagement and toil in the Babylonian Talmud specifically. Through the toil in clarifying the questions, one reaches the level of the essential concealment, as mentioned—for His Essence—and through this, one also reaches the true intent of deciding the *halachah* in practice, as mentioned, and through the action in practice (according to the *halachic* ruling), a dwelling is created for Him, may He be blessed, in the lower worlds. This is "He has made me dwell in darknesses" (the Babylonian Talmud), for through the "darknesses" (the toil in the multitude of questions, etc.) comes "He has made me dwell," a dwelling for Him, may He be blessed, in the lower worlds.

It is the same with the performance of *mitzvos* during the time of exile specifically. Although the *mitzvos* during the time of exile are not in ultimate perfection (for in addition to the fact that the primary perfection of the performance of *mitzvos* will be in the days of Mashiach, as it is written, "And there (specifically) we will perform before You, etc., according to the command of Your will," which perfection did not exist even during the time of the Beis Hamikdash, the *mitzvos* of the time of exile do not even have the perfection that existed during the time of the Beis Hamikdash), nevertheless, through the contemplation of the very fact that during the time of exile perfection is lacking, through this, the performance of the *mitzvos* is with greater strength. This is similar to what is explained in *Tanya* regarding the superiority of the *baal teshuvah* over *tzaddikim*: because he was distant from G-dliness, his thirst is with greater intensity, with great love from the depths of the heart.

Through this very fact (that the performance of *mitzvos* during the time of exile is with greater strength), one clarifies the darkness of the exile and transforms it into holiness, similar to what is explained in *Tanya* that through *teshuvah* from great love and thirst, willful sins are transformed into merits (at first, willful sins become as merits with the *kaf* of comparison, and eventually they become merits in reality). This is why the ultimate perfection of the future is accomplished (primarily) through the performance of *mitzvos* during the time of exile specifically, and especially through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. For the two matters in a dwelling in the lower worlds (which is the perfection of the future), a dwelling for His Essence and a dwelling in the lower worlds, are accomplished (primarily) through the performance of *mitzvos* during the time of exile specifically, and especially through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. Through the thirst and the greater strength during the time of exile, and especially through the cry of *teshuvah* from the depths of the heart, one draws from the depth and inwardness of above, for His Essence, and through the clarification and transformation of the darkness of the exile, and especially the transformation of willful sins into merits (through *teshuvah*), one clarifies and refines even the lowest matters, and they become a dwelling for Him, may He be blessed.

This is why the ultimate perfection of the world will be in the future specifically, "These are the generations of Peretz." This perfection is higher than the perfection that was at the beginning of creation when the world was created in its fullness, and higher even than the perfection of the world that was at the giving of the Torah. Although even at the giving of the Torah, "the world cried out 'I am,' 'You shall have no other gods'" (which are the generalities of the entire Torah), at the giving of the Torah, this was primarily due to the revelation from above (that the commandments "I am" and "You shall have no other gods" were going forth and being heard from all six directions of the world), as mentioned. Conversely, in the future, this will be in the world from its own self, as our Sages say: "In the future, if a man goes to pick a fig on Shabbos, it cries out and says, 'It is Shabbos today,' etc. The stone cries out and says, etc." (which will be from the perspective of the fig and the stone).

Furthermore, through our deeds and our *avodah* during the time of exile in the engagement in Torah and the performance of *mitzvos*, through which we clarify also the darkness of the exile, and especially through *teshuvah* where willful sins become as merits, through this, there will be a refinement of the world in the future even in the lowest matters, to the point that even the darkness will be transformed into light. This is "These are the generations of Peretz"—this is Mashiach, of whom it is said, "The breaker is gone up before them"—that he will break all the boundaries, meaning that even the boundaries (from the perspective of holiness) will be broken.

Since there must be order in the *avodah* of man, therefore, even the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, despite being above order and sequence, has a special time: the forty days between the first of Elul and Yom Kippur, which are the four *yuds* of the end of the words "I am to my Beloved and my Beloved is to me." The *avodah* of *teshuvah* is from below to above; first "I am to my Beloved," and then "my Beloved is to me." And although the last ten days of these forty days (the ten days between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur) are by way of drawing down and revelation from above to below, as our Sages say on the verse, "Seek Hashem when He is found, call upon Him when He is near"—these are the ten days between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur (for "when He is found" and "when He is near" is a revelation from above to below), and as is known that in these days there is a drawing near of the Luminary to the spark, nevertheless, these days also belong to the *avodah* of *teshuvah* that is from the side of the "below." For the drawing down and revelation from above in these days is accomplished through the *avodah* and the *is’arusa d’letata* in the month of Elul, for through the *avodah* in the first three *yuds* ("I am to my Beloved" and "my Beloved"), the *yud* of the word "to me" is created—the drawing near of the Luminary to the spark.

In these ten days, there are many ascents, up to the ultimate ascent of Yom Kippur. On Yom Kippur itself, the ultimate ascent is in the *Neilah* prayer (at the conclusion and seal of the day). It can be said that in *Neilah* itself—in the recitation of *Shema Yisrael* (which is at its conclusion and seal), and as it is found in the *Shelah* that one must intend then to surrender his soul for the sanctification of the Name, and the intention is considered for him as if he had done so in practice (perhaps it can be said that this is similar to the Baal Shem Tov’s teaching: "In the place where a man’s thought is, there he is"). All this is ascents from below to above.

Afterward begins the matter of the drawing down from above to below (through which the intent of "to dwell in the lower worlds" is completed). The beginning of the drawing down is in the four days between Yom Kippur and Sukkos, when all of Yisrael are occupied with *mitzvos*—this one is occupied with his *sukkah*, and this one with his *lulav*. Afterward, during the days of Sukkos themselves, through the performance of the *mitzvah* of sitting in the *sukkah* and taking the four species in practice, for through the performance of the *mitzvah* of *sukkah*, the drawing down is in the level of *makif* (encompassing), and through taking the four species, it is drawn down into the inwardness—"And you shall take for yourselves."

All this must be in the level of "absorption," which is accomplished on Shemini Atzeres; *Atzeres* is from the word *klitah* (absorption). (This is also what is meant by "Blow the shofar on the month, at the covering for the day of our festival," and the interpretation is known: that which is on Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur is in a manner of "at the covering" (covered and hidden), and it is revealed for the day of our festival on the festival of Sukkos. And yet, it says "the day of our festival" in the singular (and not "the days of our festival" in the plural), because the primary revelation (of the matters of Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur) in a manner of absorption is on Shemini Atzeres, "the day of our festival" in the singular.) As is known, on Shemini Atzeres is the *zivug* (union), to the point of "and they shall be one flesh," from which the birth of Torah and *mitzvos* is created ("the generations of *tzaddikim* are good deeds") throughout the entire year, to create for Him, may He be blessed, a dwelling in the lower worlds. This is also why *Atzeres* is from the word for kingship (like "he shall restrain (*ya'atzor*) my people"), for on Shemini Atzeres, a Jew becomes a king and a master over the entire chain of progression, to make of it a dwelling for Him, may He be blessed.

This is "On the eighth day, it shall be an Atzeres for you." The fact that it is called *Shemini* Atzeres indicates that it follows the seven days of Sukkos specifically, even though on Shemini Atzeres the matters of Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur are also gathered and absorbed. For on Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur, it is the matter of ascent from below to above, and since the intent is "to dwell in the lower worlds"—drawing down from above to below—therefore it is called Shemini Atzeres, in which all the matters that were drawn down from above to below during the seven days of Sukkos are gathered and absorbed. That is why it is called "Shemini" specifically (in addition to the fact that *Shemini* is from the word for fat and richness).

However, Shemini Atzeres is a festival in its own right, because through the very fact that on Shemini Atzeres the matters of the seven days of Sukkos are gathered and absorbed, a new thing is created in it: a festival in its own right. For during the seven days of Sukkos, the matters of Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur and the general Ten Days of Repentance are drawn down (in the level of *makif*) up to the perfection of the *teshuvah* of Yom Kippur. Through the drawing down of this matter into the inwardness and into absorption on Shemini Atzeres—"the day of our festival" in the singular—the connection and unification of the level of *yechidah* in the soul with the level of the *Yachid* is revealed (and in a manner of absorption), to which the matter of *teshuvah* reaches—"one bull, one ram."

This is also "It shall be an Atzeres for you." The Midrash states, "This is what is written: 'They shall be for you alone, and no strangers with you.'" The fact that it must be excluded and said "no strangers with you" is because the *baal teshuvah* whose inclination is evil needs extra caution, and therefore one must exclude the matter of "strangers" (undesirable matters). But through this very fact (the extra caution of the *baal teshuvah*), "they shall be for you alone" is created, for through this, the *teshuvah* reaches His Essence, may He be blessed, and there is no room there for any strangers—"Yisrael and the King alone." On the contrary, from the perspective of the drawing down of the Essence (through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*), the inwardness and true intent of the performance of the undesirable matters is revealed, which was by prior intent (as is known regarding "He is terrible in His doing toward the children of men") so that there would be "turning darkness into light," the transformation of willful sins into merits.

This is also why Simchas Torah was established on Shemini Atzeres specifically for the second Luchos (for the revelation of the second Luchos (the matter of *teshuvah* of Yom Kippur) in inwardness and absorption is on Shemini Atzeres, as mentioned). For the joy of Simchas Torah is (primarily) due to the fact that the Torah also affects the lowest matters in the world; this is why the joy of Simchas Torah is in dances with the feet, and within them, in the heel. Therefore, the joy is for the second Luchos specifically, the matter of *teshuvah*, for through this, even the lowest part of the world is clarified, to the point that even willful sins are transformed into merits.

Just as in a dance (physically), through the feet and heels being lifted and dancing, they lift also the head (and the power of the intellect within it), so that it too dances with great joy above measurement and limitation, so too in the spiritual matters: through the joy and dances of Simchas Torah, an ascent and lifting is created also in the level of the "head" of the soul. Since man is "an image of the Above," through this, an ascent and lifting is created, as it were, also in His Wisdom, may He be blessed, which is the Torah (as is also understood from the fact that in the dances of the *hakafos*, the Torah itself is happy and dances, as per the well-known saying of our Rebbe, the Nasi).

"Joy breaks through boundaries," for the joy

## With the help of Hashem. Simchas Torah, 5741

On the eighth day, he sent the people away, and they blessed the king and went to their tents, joyful and glad of heart for all the goodness that Hashem had done for David His servant and for Israel His people. This verse serves as the conclusion and seal of the Haftorah of Shemini Atzeres. From this, we understand that although "the king sent the people away, and they blessed the king..." was, in its literal sense, a one-time event following the dedication of the Beis Hamikdash [unlike subsequent years, where we do not find that they needed to take leave of the king (and bless him) to go from the Beis Hamikdash to their tents], the concept in a spiritual sense applies to Shemini Atzeres every single year. As explained in Likutei Torah and in several maamarim on this subject, Shemini Atzeres is the *klitah* (absorption—*Atzeres* comes from the word *klitah*) of all the matters of the month of Tishrei, beginning with the concept of *tamlichuni aleichem* (make Me king over you) from Rosh Hashanah. Indeed, one of the interpretations of *Atzeres* is related to kingship (*meluchah*), as in "this one shall restrain (*ya'atzor*) my people". From this, we understand that the concept of *Malchus* is one of the primary themes of Shemini Atzeres. This is why the matter of "the king sent the people away" occurs on Shemini Atzeres every year: on Shemini Atzeres, the acceptance of the yoke of the Kingdom of Heaven is drawn down for the entire year.

In Divrei Hayamim, it is written, "And on the twenty-third day of the seventh month, he sent the people away..." The Tzemach Tzedek explains in his glosses to Likutei Torah on this subject that this alludes to the second day of Yom Tov in the Diaspora (the second day of Shemini Atzeres is the 23rd of Tishrei). We must understand: Shemini Atzeres contains many themes; why is the allusion to the second day of Shemini Atzeres specifically connected to the matter of sending the people away?
